subject:
Re: Combining WARP projects, acquired on different days with
 different gainReferences?
body:
Hi Digvijay,

When processing in M, are you adding each dataset individually as a data
source? I think it's unwise to combine your data in Warp before this point
although I guess from a technical standpoint they are probably only dealing
with the gain reference when refining the movie frames in M. For datasets
with multiple collections, I preprocess each dataset in Warp separately to
subtomo extraction. I normally merge the particles together in Relion,
although on occasion have processed separately to a reasonable resolution
(e.g. 20A). Once I'm finished in Relion for the time being, I create an M
project and add each of my datasets as a source. Assuming the .tomostar
files are unique to each dataset, M should be able to handle the star file
from Relion. I don't know for certain, but I assume that some CTF
refinements, e.g. trefoil, tetrafoil, will be done on a per-dataset basis
so these would be less accurate if everything was merged prior.

Cheers,

Mat

On Mon, 15 Jan 2024 at 00:18, Digvijay Singh <digvijay.in1@gmail.com> wrote:

> Just to clarify; by combining, I meant combining projects and their
> tomostars that have been preprocessed, including gain and motion
> correction, already in WARP.
>
> On Sunday, January 14, 2024 at 1:44:52 PM UTC-8 Digvijay Singh wrote:
>
>> hi folks,
>>
>>    1. do you ever combine your WARP projects, acquired on different
>>    days, with possibly quite different gain references into a single WARP
>>    project folder?
>>    2. The logs in the command prompt during subtomo creation suggest
>>    that WARP loads gainReference when creating subtomos. I am not sure if it
>>    actually loads it. If it does, then does it load from the gainPath in
>>    .options file or .xml files. I notice that .xml files may not even store
>>    gainPath in many cases. Maybe WARP is able to create subtomos from
>>    ‘averaged’ frames in the averages folder. E.g., I seem to remember that
>>    renaming the average folder in the WARP project prevents subtomo
>>    generation, so maybe WARP does use these gain-corrected ‘averaged’ frames
>>    for subtomo generation.
>>    3. So the question is does WARP load and need gainReference when
>>    creating subtomos? If yes, then does it load the same gainReference for all
>>    subtomos or can figure out the right gain reference for each micrographName
>>    or tomostar of different projects, with different gainReferences when
>>    projects are combined?
>>    4. I like to keep separate WARP projects for separate microscopy
>>    sessions and then create subtomos for them separately and then I combine
>>    their star files for refinement in Relion. This works well for Relion, but
>>    to run M, I think it would be best if a single M project, combining all
>>    tomostars, can be run from a single project directory with all tomostars
>>    combined.
>>    5. To test if combining projects works well; I did combine projects
>>    and it works well. But I want to doubly make sure that combining and then
>>    creating subtomos from the combined project folder is fine.
>>
>>
>> Thanks and cheers,
>> Digvijay
>>
> --
